Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log The goal produce a process tree that captures the process behavior

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All start with RA Receive Application Then VF Validate Form Then AE Assess Eligibility

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then sometimes a loop RMI Request Missing Info and then possibly VF Validate Form again repeated zero or more times

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: There is also the possibility to go directly from AE to AD ie skip RMI and VF loop as seen in case 2

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the main process Sequence of RA to VF to AE to Loop to AD to SC to AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But also we have potential concurrency No concurrency observed So it is sequential

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Thus we need to construct a process tree capturing this

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Inside loop the loop operator with body A equals RMI and B equals VF Usually the loop operator works as do body A then either exit or do B then A again In this case we want the loop to be RMI maybe repeated then VF once after each set of RMI but the loops definition does not quite match

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: So the loop representation as given is insufficient to model repeating A before B We need to incorporate another structure

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: One way Use XOR with tau to choose either skip the repetition or execute a looped structure

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If we set A as Sequence RMI VF and B as tau then the loop would produce RMI VF then exit or RMI VF RMI VF and so on

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That matches the pattern zero or more occurrences of RMI VF Also we can have zero occurrences via XOR

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus process tree Sequence RA VF AE XOR tau Loop Sequence RMI VF tau AD SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 2 no RMI we skip loop entirely In case 1 single RMI and VF loop executes once In case 3 two RMI and VF pairs loop executes twice Thus this process tree correctly captures observed behavior
